name: Looper
description:
This algorithm is a looper, based on the disting EX algorithm of the same name, but significantly extended. Loops can have crossfades, they can fade in and out, and overdubbing can fade in and out, all of which is designed to make it easy to achieve smooth, ambient looping.

All looping operations can also be synchronised to a clock input, if tight rhythmic looping is more your thing. The looper can also generate clocks (analogue and/or MIDI), if you prefer the looper to be the source of tempo in your setup.

Loops may also be reversed, and played at half speed (an octave down).

A ‘layers’ system is also implemented, which is like the ‘undo/redo’ found on some loopers but more powerful.

The core control setup is a two button record/play/overdub paradigm in the manner of many stompbox loopers, though many more controls are available. These can be mapped to CV, MIDI, or I2C as usual; a dedicated MIDI-note based interface is also provided for ease of use with MIDI pedalboards which send notes when footswitches are pressed.

parameters.name: Target loop
parameters.description: Sets the target loop(s).

parameters.name: Record
parameters.description: Activates record or overdub.

parameters.name: Play
parameters.description: Activates play or stop.

parameters.name: Reverse
parameters.description: Activates ‘reverse’ (toggles the target loops between playing forwards and backwards).

parameters.name: Octave down
parameters.description: Activates ‘octave down’ (toggles the target loops between playing at normal speed and half speed).

parameters.name: Retrigger
parameters.description: Activates retrigger.

parameters.name: Stop all
parameters.description: Activates ‘stop all’. All currently playing loops are selected as targets, and stopped.

parameters.name: Bypass clock
parameters.description: While this parameter is held, commands such as Record, Play, etc. behave as if the ‘Clock source’ parameters were ‘None’.

parameters.name: Undo
parameters.description: Activates ‘Undo’. See Layers, above.

parameters.name: Redo
parameters.description: Activates ‘Redo’. See Layers, above.

parameters.name: Target 1
parameters.description: Activates loop 1 as a target loop.

parameters.name: Target 2
parameters.description: Activates loop 2 as a target loop.

parameters.name: Target 3
parameters.description: Activates loop 3 as a target loop.

parameters.name: Target 4
parameters.description: Activates loop 4 as a target loop.

parameters.name: Target next
parameters.description: Activates the loop after the current target as the new target.

parameters.name: Fade to clear
parameters.description: Activates ‘fade to clear’ – the same as pressing Play to stop the loops, except that the loops will be automatically cleared when the fade out has completed.

parameters.name: Pause/mute
parameters.description: Controls what happens when a loop is stopped.

parameters.name: Attack time
parameters.description: The loop envelope attack time. The scale is exponential, from 1ms to 30s.

parameters.name: Decay time
parameters.description: The decay envelope attack time. The scale is exponential, from 1ms to 30s.

parameters.name: Attack shape
parameters.description: The loop envelope attack stage shape.

parameters.name: Decay shape
parameters.description: The loop envelope decay stage shape.

parameters.name: Overdub attack time
parameters.description: The overdub envelope attack time. The scale is exponential, from 1ms to 30s.

parameters.name: Overdub decay time
parameters.description: The overdub envelope attack time. The scale is exponential, from 1ms to 30s.

parameters.name: Overdub fade
parameters.description: Sets how much the previous loop content will fade out on each pass while overdubbing.

parameters.name: Retrigger fade
parameters.description: Sets the duration of the crossfade applied when retriggering.

parameters.name: Layer fade
parameters.description: Sets the duration of the crossfade when changing layers.

parameters.name: Clear hold time
parameters.description: Sets how long Record needs to be held before a Clear happens.

parameters.name: Record hold time
parameters.description: Sets how long Record needs to be held before releasing it is treated as pressing it again.

parameters.name: Next hold time
parameters.description: Sets how long ‘Target next’ needs to be held before the target loop is set to None (or all loops, if it is already None).

parameters.name: Controls wake UI
parameters.description: If on, activating any looper control wakes the UI and dismisses the screensaver, as if you’d moved an encoder/pot/button on the module itself.

parameters.name: Only record targets
parameters.description: If enabled, only loops set as targets are able to record. Other loops’ inputs are muted.

parameters.name: Clock source
parameters.description: The clock source for synchronisation – one of ‘None’, ‘Clock input’, or ‘MIDI clock’.

parameters.name: MIDI divisor
parameters.description: The MIDI clock divisor to sync to, if the ‘Clock source’ is ‘MIDI clock’.

parameters.name: MIDI numerator
parameters.description: A multiplier for the MIDI clock.

parameters.name: MIDI transport
parameters.description: If enabled, MIDI clock start will start (or retrigger) loop playback, and MIDI clock stop will stop playback.

parameters.name: Lock range
parameters.description: Specifies a time window around the clock – if a loop would naturally loop during this window, it is retriggered so that it begins exactly on the clock pulse.

parameters.name: 2nd press cancels
parameters.description: If enabled, issuing a command a second time while the first command is still waiting for a clock will cancel the command.

parameters.name: PPQN (out)
parameters.description: The number of pulses per quarter note for output clocks. Set this to ‘24’ when generating MIDI clock.

parameters.name: Output to breakout
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the breakout.

parameters.name: Output to Select Bus
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to the Select Bus.

parameters.name: Output to USB
parameters.description: Enables MIDI output to USB.

parameters.name: Output to internal
parameters.description: Enables internal MIDI output – that is, MIDI is sent to the other algorithms.

parameters.name: Channels
parameters.description: Selects stereo or mono operation.

parameters.name: Bit depth
parameters.description: Selects ‘32 bit (float)’, ‘24 bit’, or ‘16 bit’.

parameters.name: Crossfade time
parameters.description: Sets the loop crossfade time. The scale is exponential, from 0.5ms to 5s.

parameters.name: Minimum layers
parameters.description: The minimum number of layers the loop should have, or ‘0’ for ‘Layers disabled’.

parameters.name: Current layer
parameters.description: The current layer for the loop.

parameters.name: 1/4 notes (out)
parameters.description: Length of the loop in quarter notes. Used to determine the rate at which to output clocks.

parameters.name: Input left/mono
parameters.description: The input bus for the left (if stereo) or mono signal.

parameters.name: Input right
parameters.description: The input bus for the right signal, if stereo.

parameters.name: Output left/mono
parameters.description: The output bus for the left (if stereo) or mono signal.

parameters.name: Output right
parameters.description: The output bus for the right signal, if stereo.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector for the loop outputs.

parameters.name: Trigger mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector for the trigger output.

parameters.name: MIDI channel
parameters.description: The MIDI channel on which to receive notes.

parameters.name: MIDI Target 1
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Target 1’.

parameters.name: MIDI Target 2
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Target 2’.

parameters.name: MIDI Target 3
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Target 3’.

parameters.name: MIDI Target 4
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Target 4’.

parameters.name: MIDI Target next
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Target next’.

parameters.name: MIDI Record
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Record’.

parameters.name: MIDI Play
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Play’.

parameters.name: MIDI Reverse
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Reverse’.

parameters.name: MIDI Octave down
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Octave down’.

parameters.name: MIDI Retrigger
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Retrigger’.

parameters.name: MIDI Stop all
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Stop all’.

parameters.name: MIDI Bypass clock
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Bypass clock’.

parameters.name: MIDI Undo
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Undo’.

parameters.name: MIDI Redo
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Redo’.

parameters.name: MIDI Fade to clear
parameters.description: The MIDI note to automate ‘Fade to clear’.

input_ports.name: Input left/mono
input_ports.description: The input bus for the left (if stereo) or mono signal.

input_ports.name: Input right
input_ports.description: The input bus for the right signal, if stereo.

input_ports.name: Clock input
input_ports.description: The input bus to use as clock if the ‘Clock source’ is ‘Clock input’.

output_ports.name: Output left/mono
output_ports.description: The output bus for the left (if stereo) or mono signal.

output_ports.name: Output right
output_ports.description: The output bus for the right signal, if stereo.

output_ports.name: Trigger output
output_ports.description: The output bus for an end-of-loop trigger pulse or clock.

output_ports.name: Trigger output
output_ports.description: The output bus for an end-of-loop trigger pulse or clock.
